Good, reverted back to every 10 minutes. Now I need to create a separate daily reporting task. But wait - I should first understand what the user wants:

- The heartbeat task should continue running every 10 minutes (the actual execution)
- But the delivery/report should only happen once per day

This is tricky because the current job does both execution and delivery in one. Options:
1. Disable delivery on the 10-min job, create a new daily job that just reports
2. Use conditional logic inside the job (complex)

The simplest approach: Keep the 10-min job running but set delivery to "none", then create a new daily job that runs the same script but with delivery enabled.

Actually, looking at the cron job more carefully - the user just wants the announcement to the group to happen daily, not the actual execution. So I should:
1. Keep the 10-min job running (every 10 min execution)
2. Disable delivery on this job (delivery.mode = "none")
3. Create a new daily job that just announces/report the status

Let me update the existing job to disable delivery, then create a new daily reporter.

| 任务 | 频率 | 行为 |
|------|------|------|
